22.reason: This semicolon ends the while loop, so the following block only executes once with the last line read, missing all previous lines.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  This semicolon ends the while loop prematurely, so the following block only executes once with the last line read.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  This semicolon ends the while loop, so the following block only executes once with the last line read, missing all previous lines.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Semicolon terminates loop prematurely, causing only the last line to be processed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon ends the loop, making the following block execute only once after the loop, so only the last line is processed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon here causes the loop to consume all input without processing it. The block below executes only once with empty or uninitialized data.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon here causes the loop to consume all input without processing it. The block below executes only once with whatever remained in txt (likely empty or last line), causing most words to be missed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The semicolon after the while loop causes the loop body to be empty, so the file is read but not processed. The subsequent block is executed only once after EOF, using uninitialized or leftover content in `txt`.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The semicolon after the while loop causes the loop body to be empty, so the file is read but not processed. The subsequent block is executed only once after EOF, using uninitialized or leftover content in `txt`.]

29.reason: This sets the null terminator at the wrong position; it should be after the current character, not ahead. This causes the string to be incorrectly terminated.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  This sets the null terminator at the wrong position; it should be after the current character, not ahead. This causes the string to be incorrectly terminated.

48.reason: After the for loop, the last word (if line ends with a letter) is not added. Need to handle remaining characters.
